hypothesis: A heterogeneous ensemble composed of diverse GNN architectures (e.g., GCN, GIN, GatedGCN, PaiNN) trained with the ensemble-consensus semi-supervised objective using 10% labeled QM9 and a large external unlabeled library (e.g., ZINC) will achieve a statistically significant (>5% relative) reduction in MAE and a >10% relative reduction in expected calibration error (ECE) on an out-of-domain labeled test set (e.g., OMOL25) compared to a homogeneous 4-member PaiNN ensemble trained with the same protocol (p < 0.05).
